items.title: ISRO hot-tests semi-cryogenic rocket engine power head
items.description: ISRO successfully ran a hot test of its semi-cryogenic engine power head at 175 tons thrust, reaching 88% of the target level — a key milestone for India's next-generation launch propulsion.

items.title: INS Sudarshini docks in Baltimore during Lokayan 26 voyage
items.description: The Indian Navy's sail training ship INS Sudarshini reached Baltimore as part of its Lokayan 26 expedition, strengthening naval ties with the US ahead of America's 250th-anniversary celebrations.

items.title: RRB opens document upload window for Technician Gr III candidates
items.description: Railway Recruitment Boards activated the document-upload link for CEN 02/2025 Technician Grade III candidates shortlisted after document verification, effective from June 30.

items.title: PM Modi's France and Slovakia visits precede G7 outreach session
items.description: PM Modi's tour included a strategic partnership push with France on AI and nuclear energy, the first Indian PM visit to Slovakia since 1993, and participation in the G7 outreach at Evian-les-Bains.

items.title: Padma Awards 2026 honour sportspersons including Rohit Sharma
items.description: President Droupadi Murmu conferred Padma Shri on cricketer Rohit Sharma and hockey players Savita Punia and Baldev Singh, while tennis legend Vijay Amritraj received the Padma Bhushan.

items.title: RRB NTPC Undergraduate CBT 1 exam concludes final phase
items.description: The last leg of the RRB NTPC Undergraduate CBT 1 exam under CEN 07/2025, covering 3,058 vacancies, concluded on June 20 after being conducted across multiple shifts since May.

items.title: IAF fighter squadron strength remains critically low
items.description: The Indian Air Force's active fighter squadron count has fallen to 29–31, well short of the 42-squadron benchmark needed for a two-front contingency, driven by the retirement of older aircraft.

items.title: RRB declares NTPC Graduate-level CBT 1 result
items.description: The Railway Recruitment Board announced CBT 1 results for NTPC Graduate-level posts under CEN 06/2025, shortlisting qualified candidates for the CBT 2 stage scheduled in July.

items.title: India demonstrates layered ballistic missile defence capability
items.description: DRDO conducted three consecutive flight tests demonstrating multi-layered defence against long-range ballistic missile threats and a medium-range anti-ship capability.

items.title: RRB NTPC Graduate-level CBT 2 exam date fixed for July 10
items.description: The Railway Recruitment Board announced that the NTPC Graduate-level CBT 2 exam will be held on July 10, 2026, for candidates who cleared CBT 1, with city slips out from July 1.

items.title: RBI holds repo rate at 5.25%, trims GDP growth forecast
items.description: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% for a second straight meeting, lowered the FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% and raised the inflation projection to 5.1%.

items.title: RCB retain IPL 2026 title, Sooryavanshi sweeps individual awards
items.description: Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Gujarat Titans to win back-to-back IPL titles, while 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest-ever Orange Cap winner with 776 runs.

items.title: DRDO's RudraM-II missile completes key flight trials
items.description: DRDO's RudraM-II air-to-surface missile cleared a fresh round of flight tests, advancing it toward becoming a cornerstone of the IAF's precision strike capability.

items.title: BCCI Naman Awards 2026 honour Gill, Mandhana and Dravid
items.description: Shubman Gill and Smriti Mandhana won the Best International Cricketer awards at the BCCI Naman Awards, while Rahul Dravid and Roger Binny received the Lifetime Achievement honour.
